meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Founders: SaaS Pricing Strategy to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome 'no project ideas' by validating SaaS pricing models first. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build profitable products.
content.answer_block: Stop searching for 'ideas' and start hunting for 'pricing friction.' The most profitable SaaS products solve expensive, recurring problems. By mapping high-value workflows to aggressive pricing tiers first, you reveal hidden market gaps. Use lean MVPs to test willingness-to-pay before writing a single line of core application logic.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. Most founders lose months chasing 'cool' ideas that have zero commercial viability, eventually burning out when the market responds with silence. You aren't suffering from a lack of creativity; you are suffering from a lack of market-validated constraints.

True innovation happens when you invert the process. Instead of building a product and hoping for a price, you define the value threshold first. By treating pricing as a structural feature of your architecture, you transform abstract concepts into high-converting SaaS assets that solve real-world problems.

steps.title: Identify Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Don't guess prices. Identify the 'cost of inaction' for your target niche and set your entry tier at 10% of that cost. This ensures your product is seen as an investment, not an expense.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 5 competitors in the project management niche. Extract their pricing models, identify the delta between tiers, and suggest a value-based pricing structure for a micro-SaaS targeting solo-founders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate a pricing strategy table based on competitor analysis for a SaaS MVP'

steps.title: Build the Revenue-First MVP Schema
steps.description: Architect your database to support usage-based billing from day one. This gives you the flexibility to pivot between subscription and metered models without refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a SaaS MVP using Prisma. Include tables for User, Subscription, and UsageLogs, ensuring the schema supports tiered features based on Stripe integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Scaffold Prisma schema with subscription and usage tracking'

steps.title: Implement Frictionless Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Reduce the time-to-revenue by integrating Stripe Checkout with pre-built pricing tables. Avoid custom payment forms until you have recurring revenue.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Next.js API route to handle Stripe Checkout session creation. The route should dynamically fetch the price ID based on the user's selected plan level.

steps.title: Automate Value-Triggered Emails
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to an automated email sequence that reminds users of the ROI they are missing by not upgrading.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 3-part email sequence for a SaaS product that triggers when a user hits 80% of their tier limit. Focus on the value of the next tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Resend API integration for usage-based email triggers'

steps.title: Conduct A/B Price Sensitivity Testing
steps.description: Deploy two pricing variants to your landing page to measure conversion rates. Use data to determine the 'sweet spot' for your target audience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a split-testing component for a landing page that toggles between two pricing layouts using Vercel Edge Config for zero-latency switching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement A/B test logic for pricing page using Vercel Edge Config'

pro_tips: Always price your entry-level tier high enough to filter out low-value, high-support-burden customers.
Use 'anchoring' by displaying a premium enterprise tier that makes your mid-market tier look like a bargain.
Never offer a 'Free' tier unless you have a clear, automated path to convert them to paid within 14 days.
Embed usage metrics in your UI to show users exactly how much value they are consuming in real-time.
Pivot your pricing model before you pivot your product code; it is cheaper to change a number than a feature set.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on driving traffic to your landing page and monitoring the conversion rate. If you don't see a 2% conversion within 30 days, adjust the value proposition, not the price.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start building for profit. Our community provides the feedback and technical support you need to scale your SaaS from idea to exit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
